title: SIGSEV when using `--arch`/`--platform`
body:
### Issue Description

Using any non-native architecture has started raising seg faults.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

```fish
> podman run --arch=aarch64 -it alpine
[SIGSEGV]> 
```
Replacing `aarch64` with `ppc64le` or `s390x` but not `x86_64` or `i386` also causes the same error.


### Describe the results you received

A seg fault with no error message.

### Describe the results you expected

Enters a functional interactive `aarch64` shell like it used to.

### podman info output


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Just a fairly fresh fedora (non virtualised) machine.

### Additional information

I didn't try the latest release - I'm only two patch releases off and I don't see anything related in the changelog.

comments.body:
Works for me with that package installed.
```
 $  podman run --arch=aarch64 -it alpine
Resolved "alpine" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob c41833b44d91 done  
Copying config 51e60588ff done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
/ # id
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root),1(bin),2(daemon),3(sys),4(adm),6(disk),10(wheel),11(floppy),20(dialout),26(tape),27(video)
/ # exit
```

> Do you have qemu-user-static installed?

Yeah, and at the latest versions that dnf has available.

```
~> rpm -q qemu-user-static
qemu-user-static-7.0.0-15.fc37.x86_64
~> rpm -q qemu-user-static-aarch64 
qemu-user-static-aarch64-7.0.0-15.fc37.x86_64
```
Huh, I rebooted and now it's behaving normally. Apologies for the noise... :roll_eyes:
